evidence:NBE-094.title: NBE-094: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-094.description: redb documents a zero-copy, BTreeMap-based API and can return borrowed views, but its Value trait explicitly serializes with as_bytes and deserializes with from_bytes. Storage uses copy-on-write B-trees, not std::collections::BTreeMap, so 'native Rust types without serialization' is too broad.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-095.title: NBE-095: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-095.description: redb 4.1.0 documents full ACID transactions and serializable isolation; the complete local redb test suite passed 196 tests including immediate persistence, read isolation, transaction behavior, recovery-sensitive paths, and multithreading.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-096.title: NBE-096: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-096.description: redb 4.1.0 documents MVCC with one writer and multiple concurrent snapshot readers; design documentation and passing concurrency tests prove readers do not block the active writer. Writer-to-writer concurrency remains serialized.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-097.title: NBE-097: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-097.description: The pinned artifact is a Rust library crate linked into applications. It is not a compiler and exposes embedded database APIs.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-098.title: NBE-098: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-098.description: redb's Rust implementation is compatible with a musl/static target in principle, but portability is a whole-closure property. The active CodeDB/Yazelix binaries are dynamically linked glibc ELF files with Nix-store interpreter/RUNPATH entries, and the effective Rust toolchain lacked the musl std target, so the claimed portable closure is not currently proven.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-099.title: NBE-099: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-099.description: redb 4.1.0 is a transactional key-value B-tree engine. It exposes no vector coordinate, geometry, nearest-neighbor, HNSW, or distance-computation API. The current Bun-loaded ruvector Node package owns vector geometry.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-100.title: NBE-100: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-100.description: redb implements no cosine-similarity operation. Cosine is supplied by application or RuVector code; the Bun-loaded ruvector 0.2.34 package exports cosineSimilarity and native vector database APIs.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-101.title: NBE-101: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-101.description: The exact zero-copy-f32-plus-AVX-512 redb path does not exist in redb 4.1.0. A reproducible benchmark measured redb byte reads and commits plus a separate scalar application cosine loop at 384 and 4096 dimensions; microsecond results do not convert that application loop into redb functionality or prove AVX-512 use.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-102.title: NBE-102: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-102.description: The component boundary is correct: embedding models create semantic vectors while redb passively stores and retrieves caller-supplied encoded values. Distance computation and semantic meaning remain external.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-103.title: NBE-103: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-103.description: redb is an in-process single-node embedded store and exposes no network server, replication protocol, or distributed-consensus API. Distribution therefore belongs to application protocols.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-104.title: NBE-104: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-104.description: redb is key-value and has no SQL or automatic relational secondary-index facility. Qualification is required because it supports named tables, range scans, and multimap tables from which an application can explicitly build indexes.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-105.title: NBE-105: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-105.description: The local benchmark proves low redb latency on this workstation (1536-byte read p50 about 0.27 microseconds; Immediate commit p50 about 20-24 microseconds), but no claim-scoped LMDB comparator, edge-node hardware run, determinism definition, or crash/sync matrix exists. Competitive-with-LMDB remains unproved.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-106.title: NBE-106: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-106.description: redb can technically implement an embedded high-frequency scratchpad, and measured local operations are fast enough for that use class. No accepted LifeOS data-class, retention, privacy, promotion, or recovery decision makes this its current exclusive role.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-107.title: NBE-107: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-107.description: redb can store an embedding under a BLAKE3-derived key, so the mechanism is feasible. No current model-aware cache schema or implementation proves that content, model, version, configuration, invalidation, and collision handling are all covered.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-108.title: NBE-108: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-108.description: redb can back a durable application outbox or replay queue, but it is not PostgreSQL WAL and no current ordered/idempotent redb-to-PostgreSQL outage protocol is implemented. WAL, outbox, queue, buffer, and authority semantics must not be conflated.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-109.title: NBE-109: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-109.description: Current CodeDB Rust code can transform supported source files into structured syntax rows and Nushell can host those rows. It is not a universal Nushell-owned AST transformer: language coverage varies and Cargo dependency rows come from cargo metadata rather than the Rust AST.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-110.title: NBE-110: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-110.description: At canonical nu_plugin origin/master 9451b20, the Rust plugin uses the Nushell MessagePack protocol but returns typed table values and has no direct redb dependency. Durable redb capture is a separate codedb CLI/BlobStore path, so no direct plugin-MessagePack-to-redb stream exists.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-111.title: NBE-111: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-111.description: An intermediate redb ingestion buffer is a technically possible target, but it is not the universal current role. Current CodeDB uses redb as a durable default source-blob store and envctl uses redb separately for migration/state; authority must be decided per data class.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-112.title: NBE-112: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-112.description: envctl commit c911286 reads exported JSONL rows that already contain embedding_minilm vectors into an in-memory RuVector index. It neither pulls CodeDB redb blocks, generates MiniLM embeddings, nor commits code and vectors to PostgreSQL.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-113.title: NBE-113: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-113.description: Reproducible local evidence supports the redb microsecond tier for the measured byte-read and Immediate-commit operations. No equivalent PostgreSQL-plus-RuVector relational/GNN benchmark was run, and Bun-loaded ruvector itself exposes native sub-millisecond vector operations, so the report's global microsecond-versus-millisecond boundary is not established.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-114.title: NBE-114: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-114.description: RuVector includes a native pgrx PostgreSQL extension that installs with CREATE EXTENSION and needs no vector-database sidecar. RuVector is broader than that extension: the current developer-facing ruvector 0.2.34 Node package loaded a standalone native N-API vector database through Bun, and no installed LifeOS PostgreSQL extension activation was proven.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-115.title: NBE-115: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-115.description: Current envctl does not select PostgreSQL branch state or reconstruct files from module_path. Its optional semantic adapter treats module_path as vector identity/metadata; current materialization is a separate CodeDB operation.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-116.title: NBE-116: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-116.description: Nix/Yazelix can compile a source directory and CodeDB can separately materialize stored files, but no current end-to-end selected-PostgreSQL-state to materialized directory to isolated Yazelix/Nix build trace exists.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-117.title: NBE-117: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-117.description: redb is passive transactional storage and has no ranking or learning semantics. Any ranking change is owned by application, model, RuVector, SONA, or AgentDB code.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-118.title: NBE-118: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-118.description: The latest developer-facing AgentDB package proves the core integration: agentdb 3.0.0-alpha.17 exposes SelfLearningRvfBackend over .rvf, wraps @ruvector/sona, enhances searches, records trajectories, accepts quality feedback, trains patterns, and a Bun live probe created an AgentDB .rvf plus native SONA learning. Qualification is required because search does not expose the internal q_* trajectory ID expected by recordFeedback; the documented hit-ID call recorded zero trajectories in the live p…
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-119.title: NBE-119: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-119.description: The latest Bun-loaded packages prove every narrower component: RVF defines model/overlay/LoRA segments, native SONA exposes Micro-LoRA, ruvllm held and switched 51 in-memory LoRA adapters, and Tiny Dancer trained and scored a FastGRNN router. They do not prove that one current AgentDB .rvf file persisted both the Micro-LoRA adapters and FastGRNN model, nor that 50+ complete ruvllm personalities hot-swap within one measured hardware footprint.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-120.title: NBE-120: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit
evidence:NBE-120.description: The defensible boundary is verified: redb can be one portable embedded storage component but is not a geometry engine, vector database, learning system, SQL authority, or complete intelligence architecture. Its elevation to the strategic LifeOS foundation remains an unrecorded owner architecture decision.
aliases: local source, test, Bun/N-API runtime, and benchmark truth audit

evidence:NBE-121.title: NBE-121: local source, test, runtime, benchmark, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-121.description: redb can technically be embedded in a hot edge runtime, and NBVERIFY-001 measured low in-process read/commit latency. The source prescribes an architecture but supplies no adopted LifeOS data-class, retention, durability, authority, privacy, promotion, or recovery decision, so it is not verified as current design.
aliases: local source, test, runtime, benchmark, and exact-fulltext truth audit

evidence:NBE-122.title: NBE-122: local source, test, runtime, benchmark, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-122.description: The exact causal claim is false for pinned redb 4.1.0: redb removed its mmap backend and uses positioned file I/O plus a userspace cache. The five-run NBVERIFY-001 benchmark proves microsecond-class measured operations and in-process use has no network hop, but neither fact rescues the asserted mmap/zero-copy mechanism or makes arbitrary thought/trace writes microsecond-safe.
aliases: local source, test, runtime, benchmark, and exact-fulltext truth audit

evidence:NBE-123.title: NBE-123: local source, test, runtime, benchmark, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-123.description: A background outbox worker can technically flush selected local records to PostgreSQL, but no current CodeDB/envctl call path implements the claimed per-agent-cycle compression and permanent commit. Thread ownership, final-record identity, sequence keys, compression/version identity, transaction scope, acknowledgment, retry, idempotency, and crash recovery remain absent.
aliases: local source, test, runtime, benchmark, and exact-fulltext truth audit

evidence:NBE-124.title: NBE-124: local source, test, runtime, benchmark, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-124.description: redb can back a durable outage queue or outbox, but it is not PostgreSQL WAL and no implemented protocol proves indefinite retention until connectivity returns. Capacity, backpressure, ordering, duplicates, conflict resolution, poison records, split brain, replay acknowledgment, and authority remain undefined.
aliases: local source, test, runtime, benchmark, and exact-fulltext truth audit

evidence:NBE-125.title: NBE-125: local source, test, runtime, benchmark, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-125.description: The exact 984-character, 12-line NotebookLM fulltext contains citation markers [1, 2], [2], and [3] but no bibliography, source identity, URL, immutable version, or cited passage. The references are therefore unavailable from this source and verify none of its technical assertions.
aliases: local source, test, runtime, benchmark, and exact-fulltext truth audit

evidence:NBE-126.title: NBE-126: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-126.description: The current CodeDB Nushell plugin is a Rust-native executable and the active source boundary is proven. Rust is an ecosystem implementation choice, not a Nushell protocol requirement, and the source's prescriptive 'should' is not an owner architecture decision.
aliases: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit

evidence:NBE-127.title: NBE-127: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-127.description: nu_plugin_codedb at canonical origin/master 9451b20 calls serve_plugin with MsgPackSerializer. The pinned Nushell plugin framework and prior installed wire capture prove the shell/plugin protocol path uses MessagePack.
aliases: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit

evidence:NBE-128.title: NBE-128: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-128.description: The pinned plugin protocol payload is MessagePack rather than JSON text, verified by source and wire capture. Qualification is required because CodeDB compatibility, import, export, metadata, and envctl-facing paths also use JSON; the no-JSON statement is true only at the plugin wire boundary.
aliases: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit

evidence:NBE-129.title: NBE-129: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-129.description: A reproducible 200-run, 1000-row benchmark in NBVERIFY-030-032 measured MessagePack materially faster and smaller than JSON for the pinned serializer. The current CodeDB SimplePluginCommand path still eagerly materializes input/output Values and does not prove zero-copy, bounded-memory, or direct streaming, so the full claim is too broad.
aliases: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit

evidence:NBE-130.title: NBE-130: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-130.description: The FlexNetOS/nu_plugin repository contains a durable redb-backed codedb CLI/BlobStore with tests, but the nu_plugin_codedb binary itself has no redb dependency and returns Nushell Values. The repository works with redb; the plugin process does not directly persist to it.
aliases: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit

evidence:NBE-131.title: NBE-131: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-131.description: No new PostgreSQL plugin is necessary merely to add persistence because the existing CodeDB CLI already has a direct PgStore backend. That fact contradicts the source's redb-to-envctl rationale, and no owner decision has adopted the proposed indirect boundary.
aliases: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit

evidence:NBE-132.title: NBE-132: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-132.description: Canonical nu_plugin origin/master does not buffer parsed code blocks from the plugin into redb. The separate codedb capture CLI stores raw relative-path/source-byte blobs through BlobStore; parsed syntax rows remain plugin output.
aliases: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit

evidence:NBE-133.title: NBE-133: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-133.description: envctl c911286 has no automatic nu_plugin-redb-to-PostgreSQL synchronization bridge. Its default-off semantic adapter consumes exported JSONL rows that already contain embeddings and builds an in-memory RuVector index.
aliases: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit

evidence:NBE-134.title: NBE-134: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-134.description: envctl does not permanently commit synchronized CodeDB blocks into PostgreSQL. Existing PostgreSQL writes belong to CodeDB's separate direct PgStore backend, which targets a dedicated table and is selected as an alternative to redb.
aliases: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit

evidence:NBE-135.title: NBE-135: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit
evidence:NBE-135.description: The exact 837-character NotebookLM fulltext contains markers [1], [2], [3], and [4] but no bibliography, identities, URLs, versions, or cited passages; the absent references verify none of the technical claims.
aliases: local source, installed-wire, benchmark, current-call-path, and exact-fulltext truth audit

file:planning-spine-v0/00_NORTH_STAR.md.title: 00 North Star
file:planning-spine-v0/00_NORTH_STAR.md.description: LifeOS Planning Spine v0 turns user intent into constrained, provable execution. The system is not a general org chart, not a broad agent platform, and not a simulation playground. It is a narrow operating spine that converts an intention into a verified next action through a strict chain of authority, planning, simulation, execution, and proof.

file:planning-spine-v0/01_OBJECT_MODEL.m.title: 01 Object Model
file:planning-spine-v0/01_OBJECT_MODEL.m.description: The planning spine is built from these machine-readable entities:

file:planning-spine-v0/02_AUTHORITY_GRAP.title: 02 Authority Graph
file:planning-spine-v0/02_AUTHORITY_GRAP.description: The authority graph answers a single question: who is allowed to decide, delegate, execute, and verify each planning object.

file:planning-spine-v0/03_TASK_GRAPH_SCH.title: 03 Task Graph Schema
file:planning-spine-v0/03_TASK_GRAPH_SCH.description: The task graph is the executable backbone of v0. It captures ordering, authority, constraints, rollback, proof, and simulation-derived restrictions.

file:planning-spine-v0/04_WORLDSEED_SCHE.title: 04 WorldSeed Schema
file:planning-spine-v0/04_WORLDSEED_SCHE.description: `WorldSeed` is the exact simulation input package passed to DevWorld. It freezes the task context so simulation results are reproducible and can be compared across runs.

file:planning-spine-v0/05_HERMETIC_CELL_.title: 05 Hermetic Cell Contract
file:planning-spine-v0/05_HERMETIC_CELL_.description: The hermetic cell is the smallest runtime envelope allowed to execute a task. It exists to keep execution bounded, auditable, and rollback-aware.

file:planning-spine-v0/06_PROOF_LEDGER.m.title: 06 Proof Ledger
file:planning-spine-v0/06_PROOF_LEDGER.m.description: The proof ledger is the completion authority for the planning spine. It stores evidence that a task, action, artifact, or decision actually happened and passed its required gate.

file:planning-spine-v0/07_MVP_VERTICAL_S.title: 07 MVP Vertical Slice
file:planning-spine-v0/07_MVP_VERTICAL_S.description: The first implementation target is exactly this path:

file:planning-spine-v0/08_EXECUTION_GATE.title: 08 Execution Gates
file:planning-spine-v0/08_EXECUTION_GATE.description: Execution gates are hard transition checks. They are not optional diagnostics.

file:planning-spine-v0/09_OPEN_QUESTIONS.title: 09 Open Questions
file:planning-spine-v0/09_OPEN_QUESTIONS.description: 1. How should DevWorld map external simulator plugins without turning v0 into a full Mirofish runtime? 2. What should the compiled brainpack format contain beyond role, capability, and decision templates? 3. Should proof URIs be content-addressed only, or allow mutable registry aliases?
headings: DECIDE-005 — Hermes primitives as task-graph edges (POSTV0-Q2)

file:planning-spine-v0/1.0_VISION/FOUNDA.title: LifeOS Foundation Ecosystem Map
file:planning-spine-v0/1.0_VISION/FOUNDA.description: Evidence-cited built-versus-planned ownership map for Meta coordination, consolidation, vault, and RuVector OS surfaces.
headings: FOUNDATION — LifeOS Ecosystem Map (coordination fabric, consolidation engine, vault, ruvector OS)
0. Executive answers to the pointed questions
1. meta as the coordination control plane
1.3 `meta/.claude` — meta ships its OWN Claude-Code integration (BUILT)
1.4 The REAL task system = gitkb + handoff (NOT `meta_project_cli`)
2. The knowledge/memory substrate: `.kb` + `.context`, and how TEAS integrates
2.3 TEAS integration — complementary, wired into the right layers (BUILT)
2.4 ⚠️ THE OPEN SEAM — TEAS doesn't write back to gitkb
3. THE CONSOLIDATION ENGINE — how the "single lifeos app" gets built
5. ruvector as an Agentic OS, and the rvm/nix question (CORRECTION)
5.1 ruvector is architecturally a complete Agentic OS (research-stage)
5.3 Can rvm replace nix NOW? — **No** (correcting the working assumption)
6. Cognitum + the envctl secrets vault (DESIGN-LEVEL; no secret values read)
6.1 env-ctl — the "virtual credit card" credential broker (BUILT)
6.2 Cognitum — the hardware root of trust
6.4 The COGNITUM device is a *window*, not the vault
6.5 ⚠️ SECURITY HYGIENE FINDING (verified by tracking-status only, contents never read)
7. New gaps / seams surfaced this pass (adds to G1–G4 in the portability doc)

file:planning-spine-v0/1.0_VISION/FOUNDA.title: Meta Portability and Bootstrap Model
file:planning-spine-v0/1.0_VISION/FOUNDA.description: Evidence-cited model of Meta coordination, portable runtime ownership, bootstrap boundaries, and cycle escape paths.
headings: FOUNDATION — meta's Portability & Bootstrap Model (the escape from the cycle trap)
0. The problem this solves (owner's framing, verbatim intent)
3. Coordination layer — the version-free capability DAG (connection)
3.1 The graph source: `.meta.yaml` (semantic capability tokens, NOT cargo deps)
4. `meta exec` — how commands run across repos (+ the concrete gap)
4.2 ⚠️ THE GAP (a ready-but-unwired rung, not a misunderstanding)
5. Why NOT a monorepo `[workspace]` — the rejected near-miss
6. Toolchain ownership — policy + ledger, NOT a single `.tool-chain` file
7. The portable prefix (the release artifact — a prefix, not a host root)
8. The bootstrap resolution: nix/system-depth NOW → envctl-root-managed prefix LATER
8.1 NOW — host/nix-store depth is an *explicitly accepted temporary downgrade*
8.2 LATER — envctl root management (already partly built in code)
10. How TEAS (and the whole app) sit on this foundation

file:planning-spine-v0/1.0_VISION/LifeOS.title: LifeOS Master Plan — Consolidated v1 (2026-07-07)
file:planning-spine-v0/1.0_VISION/LifeOS.description: SINGLE-FILE CONSOLIDATION of the LifeOS planning surfaces on Google Drive plus their governing tables. Append-only doctrine: no source file was modified; this document is a NEW file that consolidates. Sources and dispositions are enumerated in §9 (Provenance). Scope interpretation is stated in §9.1 — "16 planning files" reconciled against observed inventory…
headings: 1\. NORTH STAR & OPERATING DOCTRINE (from Executable Spec §1, §6; ROOT doc; RULES.md)
1.5 COMPANY & CAPABILITY FRAME (ROOT doc — pristine baseline, no RUN STATE)
2\. ARCHITECTURE — THE PLANNING SPINE (Spec §2–§18, v0 boundary)
3\. RUVNET SYSTEM MAP — CONSOLIDATED CONCLUSIONS (runs 1–3; every run doc preserves the full prior-run body; the two parallel run-3 siblings are reconciled in §3.5.3)
3.5 RUN 4 — TWO COMPLEMENTARY THREADS (both consolidated; neither is a superset)
3.5.1 Census thread closures (Doc A — declares "GAP: none open" on its thread)
3.5.2 Host-substrate fit map (Doc B — observe-only run; zero mutations)
4\. UNIFIED TASK GRAPH (canonical, from both Task Graph sheets — read 2026-07-07)
4.1 Foundation / schema / gates track — status Ready (12 rows, proof\_uri EMPTY)
4.2 Execution-pipeline \+ state/runtime-proof track — status Complete (11 rows, proofs on ledger)
4.3 Parallel lanes — status Ready, proofs already materialized and now IN GIT
7\. GAPS, RISKS, CONTRADICTIONS (surfaced, not resolved silently)
9.3 Local runtime cross-references (proof surfaces cited by this plan)

headings: Why use NPM releases first for crates before [crates.io](http://crates.io) for the latest:
Why meta-ruvector looks like messy scratchpad: (napi-rs exhaust)
Source Code Location: Fall back on Crates.io
Why Meta? The GitHub Peer repo Control Plane.
LifeOS Unix Domain Sockets (UDS) via Yazelix (Zellij)
FlexNetOS/envctl repo: “acts as the bridge tool”
AgentDB Relationship Mapping, Causal Graph Verification, Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and Cypher queries
**4\. Swarm Orchestration and Temporal Prediction (ATAS & ESN)**
**6\. System Integrity and Resilience (MinCut & Witness Chains)**
**7\. Portability, Distribution, and the "Dirty Repo" Solution**

**1\. Fundamentals of Redb: A Pure-Rust Embedded Store**
**2\. The Geometry Engine: Redb in the AI Inference Stack**
**4\. High-Frequency Use Cases for Autonomous Agents**
**5\. Workspace Migration: The Bridge to PostgreSQL and RuVector**
**The Future-Proof Swarm: A Guide to Predictive Project Forecasting with Temporal Attractors**
**1\. The Chaos of Complexity: Why Software Development Needs a Forecast**
**2\. The Semantic Brain: Understanding Echo-State Networks (ESNs)**
**3\. Mapping the Unseen: Temporal Strange Attractors**
**4\. The Multiverse Engine: Ensemble Simulations and Future Timelines**
**5\. From Simulation to Reality: Deterministic Selection via COW Branching**
**6\. The Swarm Defense: MinCut and Truth Verification**
**Technical Architecture Specification: Autonomous Database-Driven Development Environment**

file:planning-spine-v0/weave_state/weave.title: WEAVE-001
file:planning-spine-v0/weave_state/weave.description: Evaluation complete. src/weave has the expected local bus primitives and heartbeat design, and `yzx` is confirmed as the installed runtime owner, but neither the clean pack prefix nor plain `yzx run` expose a weave runtime frontdoor or Rust toolchain frontdoor.

file:planning-spine-v0/worldsim/README.m.title: worldsim — Nix USD toolchain dev shell (LPS-030)
file:planning-spine-v0/worldsim/README.m.description: A hermetic Nix dev shell that provides the OpenUSD Python API, the `usdchecker` / `usdcat` / `usdzip` command-line tools, and a headless MeshLab so the worldsim ingestion pipeline (LPS-029..036) runs reproducibly on the workstation without mutating the system nix profile or `~/.local`.
aliases: worldsim — Nix USD toolchain dev shell (LPS-030)
headings: worldsim — Nix USD toolchain dev shell (LPS-030)
Or run a single gate command

structured:architecture-depth:depth-001.title: LifeOS is the living company, personal, development, and home operating environment; David remains final authority.
aliases: LifeOS is the living company, personal, development, and home operating environment; David remains final authority.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-002.title: Human intent becomes a governed, simulated, executable, verified task graph ending in a proven next-action recommendation.
aliases: Human intent becomes a governed, simulated, executable, verified task graph ending in a proven next-action recommendation.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-003.title: Every meaningful action passes through identity, policy, memory, simulation, execution, proof, and rollback; no agent receives unlimited authority.
aliases: Every meaningful action passes through identity, policy, memory, simulation, execution, proof, and rollback; no agent receives unlimited authority.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-004.title: All valid peer capabilities converge physically and upgrade-only into one grown-up LifeOS application rather than remaining a permanently orchestrated fleet.
aliases: All valid peer capabilities converge physically and upgrade-only into one grown-up LifeOS application rather than remaining a permanently orchestrated fleet.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-005.title: The consolidated application is Rust-native, uses governed Lua policy surfaces, and runs on the portable confined Yazelix plus Nix foundation.
aliases: The consolidated application is Rust-native, uses governed Lua policy surfaces, and runs on the portable confined Yazelix plus Nix foundation.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-006.title: The first self-sustaining product vertical is governed financial and legal operation.
aliases: The first self-sustaining product vertical is governed financial and legal operation.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-007.title: The first build is the governed task-execution substrate required by every later LifeOS vertical.
aliases: The first build is the governed task-execution substrate required by every later LifeOS vertical.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-008.title: The shared task contract, witnessed proof authority, execution kernel, runtime integration, and mandatory human-approval gates converge without parallel competing types.
aliases: The shared task contract, witnessed proof authority, execution kernel, runtime integration, and mandatory human-approval gates converge without parallel competing types.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-009.title: Company, development, personal, and home capabilities become governed LifeOS domains over the same consolidated substrate.
aliases: Company, development, personal, and home capabilities become governed LifeOS domains over the same consolidated substrate.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-010.title: Memory, failure learning, local inference and training, mesh compute and storage, communications, regenerative configuration, secrets, and network control form one governed intelligence substrate.
aliases: Memory, failure learning, local inference and training, mesh compute and storage, communications, regenerative configuration, secrets, and network control form one governed intelligence substrate.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-011.title: Desktop and later approved mobile, glasses, and ambient interfaces expose the same LifeOS state while the replaceable UI remains subordinate to the Rust-native substrate.
aliases: Desktop and later approved mobile, glasses, and ambient interfaces expose the same LifeOS state while the replaceable UI remains subordinate to the Rust-native substrate.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-012.title: Scope boundaries, doctrine, proof-based success, anti-drift, rollback, Ubuntu-first release, and continuous governed improvement remain enforceable end to end.
aliases: Scope boundaries, doctrine, proof-based success, anti-drift, rollback, Ubuntu-first release, and continuous governed improvement remain enforceable end to end.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-013.title: PostgreSQL plus RuVector is the target canonical editable macro-state substrate for the complete LifeOS user-space, but the exact relationship to valid current redb, nu_plugin CodeDB, AgentDB, envctl migration, secret-store, and projection use cases must be o…
aliases: PostgreSQL plus RuVector is the target canonical editable macro-state substrate for the complete LifeOS user-space, but the exact relationship to valid current redb, nu_plugin CodeDB, AgentDB, envctl migration, secret-store, and projection use cases must be owner-decided without capability loss or silent dual authority.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-014.title: The entire user-space, including planning and control tables, is represented by stable relational, temporal, semantic, provenance, permission, history, blob-reference, encrypted-secret, and graph identities; every planning question is queried and every accept…
aliases: The entire user-space, including planning and control tables, is represented by stable relational, temporal, semantic, provenance, permission, history, blob-reference, encrypted-secret, and graph identities; every planning question is queried and every accepted answer is updated in PostgreSQL.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-015.title: Archive-first collectors ingest every user-space entity losslessly through restartable staging; RuVector adds reproducible embeddings, hybrid retrieval, semantic SQL edits, copy-on-write branches, temporal alternatives, witness chains, and MinCut isolation.
aliases: Archive-first collectors ingest every user-space entity losslessly through restartable staging; RuVector adds reproducible embeddings, hybrid retrieval, semantic SQL edits, copy-on-write branches, temporal alternatives, witness chains, and MinCut isolation.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-016.title: LifeOS Mission Control reads and directs canonical database state through a local UDS contract and redb-backed low-latency views; no HTTP or editable flat-file detour becomes a parallel control plane.
aliases: LifeOS Mission Control reads and directs canonical database state through a local UDS contract and redb-backed low-latency views; no HTTP or editable flat-file detour becomes a parallel control plane.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-017.title: envctl deterministically projects a selected database branch and version into a disposable directory; Yazelix plus Nix consumes that projection to produce portable, reproducible LifeOS builds and executable closures.
aliases: envctl deterministically projects a selected database branch and version into a disposable directory; Yazelix plus Nix consumes that projection to produce portable, reproducible LifeOS builds and executable closures.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-018.title: Flat files cease to be editable authority only after whole-user-space parity, behavioral equivalence, deterministic reconstruction, backup, restore, and failback pass; preserved history remains available and projections remain traceable to database identities.
aliases: Flat files cease to be editable authority only after whole-user-space parity, behavioral equivalence, deterministic reconstruction, backup, restore, and failback pass; preserved history remains available and projections remain traceable to database identities.

structured:architecture-depth:depth-019.title: Valid existing work is an upgrade input, not a reason to rebuild: contradictory prior task tables are reconciled against source and proof; proven CodeDB, envctl migration, secret, PostgreSQL, RuVector, redb, and materialization capabilities are adopted once;…
aliases: Valid existing work is an upgrade input, not a reason to rebuild: contradictory prior task tables are reconciled against source and proof; proven CodeDB, envctl migration, secret, PostgreSQL, RuVector, redb, and materialization capabilities are adopted once; unresolved store relationships remain explicit decisions.

task:ADOPT-001.title: Existing nu_plugin CodeDB storage capability adopted without rebuild
task:ADOPT-001.description: Adopt the proven BlobStore trait, redb source-blob store, PostgreSQL PgStore, resumable capture, SHA-256 identity, exact-byte materialization, secret-output guards, bounded MCP, and envctl export capabilities as upgrade inputs rather than duplicate implementation tasks.
aliases: Existing nu_plugin CodeDB storage capability adopted without rebuild

task:ADOPT-002.title: Existing envctl migration secrets and RuVector capability adopted without rebuild
task:ADOPT-002.description: Adopt envctl's replayable migration engine, catalog blob metadata, secrets-engine, secretd, secretctl, remote libSQL store, PostgreSQL-RuVector component, and semantic consumer as valid capabilities while separating package proof, merged product proof, live runtime proof, and unresolved store authority.
aliases: Existing envctl migration secrets and RuVector capability adopted without rebuild

task:COMPANY-001.title: Company governance and operating model task family defined
task:COMPANY-001.description: Define LifeOS-native company identity, authority, priorities, projects, departments, roles, decisions, budgets, risk, compliance, and proof workflows under David's final authority.
aliases: Company governance and operating model task family defined

task:COMPANY-002.title: Company and development vertical acceptance plan defined
task:COMPANY-002.description: Define an end-to-end proof that a company goal becomes governed development work, a verified release, an observed operating outcome, and a learned next action inside LifeOS.
aliases: Company and development vertical acceptance plan defined

task:CONSOLIDATE-001.title: LifeOS target bounded-context architecture defined
task:CONSOLIDATE-001.description: Define the single-application Rust bounded contexts, stable contracts, data ownership, policy seams, and capability destinations that receive every preserved peer capability.

task:CONSOLIDATE-002.title: Upgrade-only capability migration waves defined
task:CONSOLIDATE-002.description: Define dependency-ordered migration waves that physically merge peer capabilities into LifeOS while preserving behavior, history, tests, proof, and reversible fallback until parity passes.

task:CONSOLIDATE-003.title: Capability parity cutover and legacy retirement plan defined
task:CONSOLIDATE-003.description: Define proof-gated cutover, consumer migration, installed-runtime validation, and archive-first retirement tasks for peer implementations only after their LifeOS-native capability reaches parity.
aliases: Capability parity cutover and legacy retirement plan defined

task:DECIDE-001.title: Decide: DevWorld <-> Mirofish adapter scope (RFC-Q1)
task:DECIDE-001.description: Bounded owner decision for open question RFC-Q1: how DevWorld maps external simulator plugins without turning v0 into a full Mirofish runtime. Owner: David / Governance Board. Unblock condition: David records a decision selecting adapter-only mapping or an approved alternative in 09_OPEN_QUESTIONS.md. Deferral/rollback rule: until decided, Mirofish remains adapter-only and no Mirofish runtime enters v0. North Star anchor: 00_NORTH_STAR.md boundary 'DevWorld <-> Mirofish adapter = RFC'.

task:DECIDE-002.title: Decide: compiled agent brainpack format (RFC-Q2)
task:DECIDE-002.description: Bounded owner decision for open question RFC-Q2: what the compiled brainpack format should contain beyond role, capability, and decision templates. Owner: David / Governance Board. Unblock condition: David records a decision fixing the brainpack payload or keeping it RFC-only in 09_OPEN_QUESTIONS.md. Deferral/rollback rule: until decided, brainpacks remain RFC-only and do not replace the v0 JSON Schema contracts. North Star anchor: 00_NORTH_STAR.md boundary 'Compiled agent brainpack = RFC'.

task:DECIDE-003.title: Decide: proof URI addressing model (RFC-Q3)
task:DECIDE-003.description: Bounded owner decision for open question RFC-Q3: whether proof URIs are content-addressed only or may use mutable registry aliases. Owner: David / Governance Board. Unblock condition: David records a decision selecting content-addressed-only or an approved alias policy in 09_OPEN_QUESTIONS.md. Deferral/rollback rule: until decided, proof URIs default to content-addressed-only and no mutable alias is introduced. North Star anchor: 06_PROOF_LEDGER.md proof URI / hash surface.

task:DECIDE-004.title: Decide: Odysseus role when integrated (POSTV0-Q1)
task:DECIDE-004.description: Bounded owner decision for open question POSTV0-Q1: whether Odysseus acts as a task executor, a task source, or both when added. Owner: David / Governance Board. Unblock condition: David records a decision fixing the Odysseus role in 09_OPEN_QUESTIONS.md at the point post-v0 integration is authorized. Deferral/rollback rule: until decided, Odysseus and Hermes remain out of v0. North Star anchor: 00_NORTH_STAR.md boundary 'Full Odysseus and Hermes integration = post-v0'.

verification-queue:NBV-382.title: NBV-382: YNP-CLAIM-009
verification-queue:NBV-382.description: Run local store adapter and transaction trace and require Pinned writer/adapter source, schema and key identity, transaction tests, crash/replay behavior, synchronization map, and explicit owner decision for redb authority.

verification-queue:NBV-383.title: NBV-383: YNP-CLAIM-010
verification-queue:NBV-383.description: Run end to end encoding trace and negative test and require Pinned command-to-store call graph, wire capture, serializer instrumentation, compatibility/export-path inventory, and tests that distinguish protocol payloads from unrelated JSON surfaces.

verification-queue:NBV-384.title: NBV-384: YNP-CLAIM-011
verification-queue:NBV-384.description: Run retain as question and separately trace installed runtime and require Owner-approved launch contract plus source-to-package-to-/home/flexnetos/.nix-profile/bin/yzx trace, editable-input provenance, generated-runtime receipt, process tree, readiness, failure, shutdown, and rollback.

verification-queue:NBV-385.title: NBV-385: YNP-CLAIM-012
verification-queue:NBV-385.description: Run retain as question and use canonical owner directed task selection and require Explicit owner direction linked to a committed canonical task and satisfied prerequisites.

verification-queue:NBV-386.title: NBV-386: YNP-CLAIM-013
verification-queue:NBV-386.description: Run recover immutable citation identities or record unavailable and require Reference identities, immutable versions or commits, exact cited passages, and claim-level relationship assessments.

verification-queue:NBV-387.title: NBV-387: REDB-CLAIM-001
verification-queue:NBV-387.description: Confirm exact redb crate version and build features during ADOPT-001.

verification-queue:NBV-388.title: NBV-388: REDB-CLAIM-006
verification-queue:NBV-388.description: Preserve this distinction in the store decision.

verification-queue:NBV-389.title: NBV-389: REDB-CLAIM-011
verification-queue:NBV-389.description: Name the embedding and vector-compute owners separately from storage.

verification-queue:NBV-390.title: NBV-390: REDB-CLAIM-012
verification-queue:NBV-390.description: Capture distribution and replication as application responsibilities.

verification-queue:NBV-391.title: NBV-391: REDB-CLAIM-013
verification-queue:NBV-391.description: Verify exact indexing facilities and retain SQL/relational work in PostgreSQL.

verification-queue:NBV-392.title: NBV-392: REDB-CLAIM-018
verification-queue:NBV-392.description: Map native parsers versus line-oriented fallbacks during ingestion decomposition.

verification-queue:NBV-393.title: NBV-393: REDB-CLAIM-023
verification-queue:NBV-393.description: Verify extension/client version pairing and live runtime before cutover.

verification-queue:NBV-394.title: NBV-394: REDB-CLAIM-025
verification-queue:NBV-394.description: Prove through isolated source build before profile installation.

verification-queue:NBV-395.title: NBV-395: REDB-CLAIM-026
